rulings.Case Title: SHANE BRANDON et al VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, September 30, 2011 in Department 220 at 10:00 a.m., Line 14. Defendant Lehigh Hanson, Inc.¿s motion to quash service of summons is granted. Plaintiff(s)¿s request for jurisdictional discovery is denied. Plaintiff(s) failed to make any showing of the second element of the alter ego doctrine ¿ i.e. ¿the facts are such that an adherence to the fiction of the separate existence of the corporation would, under the particular circumstances, sanction a fraud or promote injustice¿ (9 Witkin, Summary of California Law (10th ed. 2005) ¿Corporations¿ §10, quoting Minifie v. Rowley (1921) 187 Cal. 481, 487)) ¿ or that any of the jurisdictional discovery sought to be taken will produce evidence to prove that element. (See also Sonora Diamond Corp. v. Superior Court (2000) 83 Cal. App. 4th 523, 539 (alter ego doctrine not a grounds to impose personal jurisdiction where there was no evidence of any wrongdoing by either the subsidiary or the parent or any evidence of injustice flowing from the recognition of the subsidiary¿s separate corporate identity)). The most recent supplemental papers filed by plaintiff(s) merely show that plaintiffs, if entitled to do so under Civil Code section 3295, desire to take punitive damages discovery from Lehigh Hanson, not that any fraud or injustice would result from adhering to the fictional separate existence of Lehigh Hanson from Kaiser Gypsum. Plaintiff(s)¿s desire to take punitive damages discovery from Lehigh Hanson can be fully accomplished through means other than invoking the alter ego doctrine. If any party contests the tentative ruling, the hearing will be at 10am on September 30, not at 9:30am on September 29. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the court with a copy to opposing counsel by 4:30pm on September 29 by email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org. = (220/HEK)
